user_story: As a tool maintainer, I want CLI and MCP search to share one implementation so behavior stays consistent.
expected_behavior_based_on_code: qlik_search.py and qlik_mcp_server.py both call qlik_index.search and qlik_index.format_hits.
initial_result: Pass: imports ok.
errors_documented: py_compile could not update locked pycache files from prior interrupted run.
fix_applied: Used import-based syntax check to avoid generated pycache mutation; code paths import cleanly.
post_fix_retest: imports ok.

user_story: As a search user, I want useful results even when Chroma cannot open its SQLite database.
expected_behavior_based_on_code: qlik_index.search falls back to chunks.jsonl lexical retrieval on Chroma exceptions and expands common user synonyms such as upsert to corpus terms.
initial_result: Initially search survived Chroma issues, but upsert wording returned append-only before insert/update patterns.
errors_documented: Chroma disk I/O/read-only errors in this environment; lexical scorer did not understand upsert as insert/update wording.
fix_applied: Added deterministic JSONL fallback plus upsert synonym expansion to insert/update corpus terms.
post_fix_retest: Retest returned Pattern 4: Insert, Update and Delete first, then Pattern 3: Insert and Update.

user_story: As a maintainer, I want to build a local Chroma index from chunks.jsonl.
expected_behavior_based_on_code: build_index.py loads chunks and creates a Chroma collection named qlik_knowledge.
initial_result: Initially failed under workspace path with Chroma disk I/O error.
errors_documented: Workspace-local SQLite was unreliable for Chroma in this environment.
fix_applied: Changed default index location to LOCALAPPDATA cache and created parent directories before Chroma startup.
post_fix_retest: Retest built 498 chunks to C:\Users\sean_\AppData\Local\qlik-ai-skill\index\chroma_db.

user_story: As a maintainer, I want rebuilds in alternate output directories to reuse the bundled local ONNX model.
expected_behavior_based_on_code: build_index.py passes LocalONNXEmbeddingFunction a model dir, and qlik_index validates complete ONNX model files.
test_performed: Built index to a temp output directory during diagnosis.
initial_result: Initially attempted network download when alternate outdir had an incomplete model folder.
errors_documented: Incomplete outdir/model folder was treated as valid because only folder existence was checked.
fix_applied: Added _model_ready check for required ONNX files and fallback to bundled tool/model.
post_fix_retest: Retest built temp index successfully with 498 chunks and no model download.

user_story: As a CLI user, I want a query to print formatted passages with source and heading path.

initial_result: Initially failed due Chroma path/read-only and encoding; later upsert query returned less relevant append-only result first.
errors_documented: Chroma unavailable in sandbox; cp1252 UnicodeEncodeError; upsert user wording did not map to insert/update reference terminology.
fix_applied: Added JSONL fallback, UTF-8/replacement-safe CLI output, and upsert synonym expansion.
post_fix_retest: Retest returned formatted passages with source and heading path; upsert query returned insert/update patterns first.

user_story: As a new user, I want setup.py to create dependencies and build the retrieval index with one command.
expected_behavior_based_on_code: setup.py creates venv, installs requirements, regenerates chunks if missing, and builds Chroma index.
initial_result: Failed when Chroma was launched by setup against a read-only/default or workspace fallback index path in this sandbox.
errors_documented: Chroma child process returned readonly database/disk I/O errors; raw traceback leaked into setup output before fallback handling.
fix_applied: Setup now probes the default index path, attempts a workspace fallback, captures index-build output, and completes cleanly with bundled chunks.jsonl fallback when Chroma is blocked.
post_fix_retest: Retest exited 0, printed DONE, and reported JSONL fallback mode without traceback.

user_story: As a new user, I want setup.py to print an MCP registration command using the correct index path.
expected_behavior_based_on_code: setup.py prints an MCP registration command using QLIK_INDEX_DIR when Chroma build succeeds, or a fallback-mode command without QLIK_INDEX_DIR when Chroma is blocked.
initial_result: Old setup assumed a Chroma index path would always be available.
errors_documented: When Chroma build was blocked, printing a failed QLIK_INDEX_DIR would point users at a broken partial index.
fix_applied: Conditionally print QLIK_INDEX_DIR only after a successful Chroma build; otherwise print fallback-mode MCP instructions.
post_fix_retest: Retest printed fallback-mode MCP command without QLIK_INDEX_DIR.
